prior_scan_disposition.note: All 3 findings from scan-008 addressed: 'property rights' replaced with 'economic stability and family security' (f1), 'Research suggests' hedging added to personal letters claim (f2), 'mirrors' replaced with 'echoes' (f3). Clean resolution.

findings.description: Braver Angels described as 'the nation's largest cross-partisan citizen movement' without attribution. Braver Angels uses this self-description on their own website, but the page presents it as editorial fact rather than attributed claim. Fair witness standard calls for distinguishing the organization's self-characterization from independently verified status.
findings.suggestion: Attribute the claim: 'which describes itself as the nation's largest cross-partisan citizen movement' or 'reportedly the nation's largest cross-partisan citizen movement.' Alternatively, drop the superlative and describe what they do: 'a national cross-partisan citizen movement.'
findings.context: Braver Angels, the nation's largest cross-partisan citizen movement, aims to show that structured dialogue across political differences can produce common ground

epistemic_flags: Single low-severity finding on an advocacy page — the feedback loop shows healthy convergence with all prior findings resolved
Braver Angels does use 'largest cross-partisan' self-description on braverangels.org; the finding concerns attribution practice, not factual accuracy
Several candidate findings fell below the 0.7 confidence threshold — the page quality has improved substantially across the last three scan cycles
